title: BCIT Basic Guard Duty
description: The BCIT Basic Guard Duty dataset comprises EEG, eye-tracking, and behavioral data collected during a sustained vigilance task in which participants verified replica identification cards against reference images while performing a concurrent driving simulator task. The study measured cognitive and physiological responses to varying ID presentation rates (1-25 per minute) across ten 5-minute blocks, with participants making allow/deny decisions based on ID validity criteria. This dataset provides a rich resource for investigating EEG correlates of sustained attention, fatigue, and decision-making under varying cognitive workload in a realistic security screening context.
methods_description: EEG was recorded using BioSemi 256-channel systems (plus 8 additional channels including 4 eye and 2 mastoid channels) at 1024 Hz sampling rate. Eye tracking was performed using Sensomotoric Instruments REDEYE250. Participants completed a guard duty task involving serial presentation of ID cards paired with reference face images from the Multi-PIE database. The task was divided into ten 5-minute blocks with ID presentation rates varying stochastically between 1 and 25 per minute. Participants made self-paced two-alternative forced-choice responses (allow/deny) with a 20-second maximum response time, and provided reasons for denied access from a predefined list.
